Section 1

Overall funnel performance · FS+Insta

BQL is total for the 23 clusters (no channel filter). MS is total. MD / Order / HOTO are FS+Insta filtered.

Section 2

Funnel conversion rates

Pick a cluster and date range. Default: MTD across all 23 clusters. LMTD auto-mirrors same days in May.

Funnel conversion trends · daily

Daily conversion at each funnel stage — MTD (solid blue) vs LMTD same June span (light blue) vs Target (amber dashed) — plus the daily/cumulative order trend. Insights under each chart identify, on a volume-weighted basis, which clusters are pulling the PAN India curve up or down for the latest day and over the last 5 days. Select a cluster to switch to cluster-trend insights.
Channel and lead-score filters apply to all five charts; the kW filter applies to MD→Order and Order→HOTO only. The Target curve is shown only when channel, lead score, and kW are all at "All" — the plan has no lead-score or kW splits. Lead-score "All" includes unscored leads.

Order trend · MTD vs LMTD vs Target

How to read the insights (click to expand)
Curves — solid blue = this month's daily conversion; light blue = the same June date (LMTD); amber dashed = the July plan's implied conversion for that day (planned stage ÷ planned prior stage). Gaps in a curve mean zero volume in the denominator that day.
Y-axis capped at 100% — all four conversion charts use a fixed 0–100% axis so day-to-day movement around typical conversion levels stays readable. A day can genuinely exceed 100% (e.g., Order→HOTO when HOTOs from earlier orders land on a low-order day); those points are pinned at the top of the chart as ▲ triangles, and hovering shows the true value with an "above 100% — axis capped" note.
Daily vs Cumulative tabs — Daily shows each day's standalone conversion (that day's numerator ÷ that day's denominator). Cumulative shows the running month-to-date ratio through each day: e.g., cumulative BQL→MS on Jul 10 = total MS booked Jul 1–10 ÷ total BQL booked Jul 1–10. Cumulative smooths day-to-day noise and converges to the MTD figure in Section 1; Daily exposes the day-level swings that drive it. Insights below the charts always use the daily/5-day windows regardless of tab.
PAN India insights (volume-weighted) — the PAN curve is a blended average, so a cluster's pull on it = its share of that stage's volume × (its conversion − PAN conversion), expressed in percentage points (pp). A large cluster converting slightly below PAN can drag more than a small cluster converting far below it — the weighting reflects real order/meeting impact, not just conversion gaps. The contributions of all clusters sum to zero around the PAN average. We list the top 5 dragging and top 2 lifting clusters; clusters with under 5 units of stage volume in the window are excluded as noise, as are contributions under 0.05pp.
Two windows — the first line decomposes the latest completed day; the second blends the last 5 days (summing numerators and denominators, not averaging daily percentages) and states whether the latest day is improving, declining, or flat vs that 5-day blend (±0.3pp band = flat).
Cluster view — with a cluster selected, insights switch to that cluster's own trend: latest day vs its 5-day blend (improving/declining), and the 5-day blend vs its plan target (above/below, in pp).
Reading a drag number — "Kanpur −1.2pp" on MD→Order means: if Kanpur had converted at the PAN India rate that day, the PAN India curve would sit 1.2pp higher. It is the cluster's weighted contribution to the gap, not the cluster's own conversion.

Meetings and Orders · by SC Index bucket

MDs and Orders attributed to the Current SC of each lead, bucketed by their SC Index score (× 10). Filter matches Section 1 totals (FS + Insta only). "No SC score" = MDs/Orders done by SCs missing from the SC Index sheet. Conv % = orders in bucket / MDs in bucket over the selected date range. SC count = unique SCs who did ≥1 MD in that bucket over the range. MD/SC = MDs ÷ SC count in that bucket.
Color coding · MD Share (active when a specific lead-score bucket is selected; rows with <20 MDs stay uncolored)
· Lead score 7–9 / 9–10 — premium leads should route to top SCs: Score>7 share ≥45% 30–45% <30% · Score<5 / unscored share ≤15% >30%
· Lead score 0–2 / 2–5 / 5–7 — weaker leads should route to mid/low SCs: Score 5–7 & Score<5 share ≥35% <20% · Score>7 share ≤20% >35% (don't burn top SCs on weak leads)
Color coding · Conv % (all filter states, incl. All) — vs the cluster's blended conversion for the current filter: ≥1.15× 0.85–1.15× <0.85× · cells with <10 MDs stay uncolored.

Order-loss diagnosis · by channel and funnel stage

Same Shapley 4-stage waterfall as above, applied per channel: Digital / Referral / SolarPro / BTL. Channel = lead's Source_Class_Final; the same lead's stages are attributed to the same channel end-to-end. Channel-summed total will trail Section 9 by the residual in IVR / Cold Marketing / other minor channels (which are excluded here). See the methodology block at the bottom for the full formula.

PlanActualLoss BQL qtyBQL→MSMS→MDMD→Order Tgt%Act%Δpp Tgt%Act%Δpp Tgt%Act%Δpp
How this is calculated · click to expand
1. Data sources
  • BQL actuals: from the BQL Raw sheet, per day × cluster × channel (channel = Source_Class_final, col C).
  • MS/MD/Order actuals: from the Raw Data sheet, using First_Meeting_Schedule_Date (col R) for MS, First_Meeting_Done_Date or Last_Meeting_Done_Date (cols T/U) for MD, Order_Done_Date (col V) for Orders. Channel attribution: Source_Class_Final (col D) — the lead's original source is used consistently across all four stages, so per-lead conversion math is coherent.
  • Plan targets: from the July MOP planning sheet, per city × day × channel columns. BQL: [7]/[8,9]/[10,11]/[12] for Digital/Referral/SolarPro/BTL. MS: [30]/[31,32]/[33,34]/[35]. MD: [39,40]/[41,42,43]/[44,45]/[46]. Orders: [53,54]/[55,56,57]/[58,59]/[60].
2. Filter conventions (aligned to Section 9)
  • BQL — all channels, no additional filter.
  • MS — no FS/IS filter (matches ms_mtd in Section 1 and the Shapley input).
  • MD — Field Sales only. Fresh MDs where First_Sales_Channel ≠ "Inside Sales"; reactivation MDs where Last_Sales_Channel ≠ "Inside Sales". Matches md_fs in Section 1.
  • Orders — Field Sales only, where Current_Sales_Channel ≠ "Inside Sales". Matches order_fs in Section 1.
3. Shapley 4-stage waterfall (per channel)
For each channel and the selected date range, we aggregate plan and actual for BQL, MS, MD, Orders, then compute conversion rates:
conv_BQL→MS = MS / BQL
conv_MS→MD = MD / MS
conv_MD→Order = Order / MD
Now substitute one input at a time — starting from all-plan and ending at all-actual. Each step's drop in projected orders is that stage's loss:
O₀ = plan_BQL × conv_BQL→MS_plan × conv_MS→MD_plan × conv_MD→Order_plan  // = plan_orders
O₁ = actual_BQL × conv_BQL→MS_plan × conv_MS→MD_plan × conv_MD→Order_plan  // swap BQL
O₂ = actual_BQL × conv_BQL→MS_actual × conv_MS→MD_plan × conv_MD→Order_plan  // swap BQL→MS
O₃ = actual_BQL × conv_BQL→MS_actual × conv_MS→MD_actual × conv_MD→Order_plan  // swap MS→MD
O₄ = actual_BQL × conv_BQL→MS_actual × conv_MS→MD_actual × conv_MD→Order_actual  // = actual_orders

loss_BQL_quantum = O₀ − O₁
loss_BQL→MS = O₁ − O₂
loss_MS→MD = O₂ − O₃
loss_MD→Order = O₃ − O₄
Total loss = O₀ − O₄ = sum of the four losses (exact — no residual)
This is the same "LOCKED" Shapley formula used in the main Section 9 table above — just applied per channel instead of at cluster level.
4. How to read each column
  • BQL qty — orders lost because actual BQL was below plan. A large value here means "not enough leads came in for this channel."
  • BQL→MS / MS→MD / MD→Order — orders lost because the conversion rate at that stage was below plan. The Δpp columns on the right show the exact percentage-point drop.
  • A negative loss in any column means the channel is outperforming plan at that stage — a gain that offsets other losses.
  • The total loss for each channel is the sum of its four stage losses.
5. Cumulative-loss chart (time axis)
For each channel, cumulative loss on day D = (sum of plan orders through D) − (sum of actual orders through D). A steepening slope shows when the gap started widening; a flat or declining slope shows recovery. This chart uses just the order counts — not the Shapley decomposition — because "when did the loss happen" is answered by the timing of the order shortfall itself.
6. Why the channel-summed total won't match Section 9 exactly
Section 9's total includes IVR, Cold Marketing, Newspaper, and other minor channels. Only Digital / Referral / SolarPro / BTL are shown here. The gap between Section 9's total and this section's channel-summed total is the loss attributable to those excluded channels.
7. Quantum vs. mix — how to interpret
  • A pure quantum drop (fewer leads across all channels, mix unchanged) shows up as large BQL qty losses distributed across channels roughly in their plan proportions.
  • A mix shift (e.g. Referral BQLs down, Digital BQLs up) shows up as a large BQL qty loss in Referral with a negative BQL qty loss in Digital. Because Referral usually converts higher, the loss on Referral often outweighs the Digital gain — so net orders still drop even if total BQL is unchanged. The row-level BQL qty values make this visible directly; you can also spot it by comparing each channel's actual BQL share to its plan share.
  • A conversion problem shows up in the BQL→MS / MS→MD / MD→Order columns instead of the BQL qty column — the volume was there but the funnel leaked at a specific stage.

Section 17

July 2026 order projection · benchmark vs actual

Day-on-day forecast for Jul, frozen on Jun 29, 2026 from Mar–Jun history. Captures DOW (Mon low / Sun high) and week-of-month effects. Once July begins, daily actuals overlay onto the benchmark — revised projections update for remaining days based on actual run-rate.
Cluster Historical monthly totals (full count) July (selected range)
MarAprMayJun Proj (orig) Actual Proj remaining Revised total Δ vs orig To-go run-rate

July order trajectory · projected vs actual (all 23 clusters, daily)

How this projection is built · methodology

1. Cluster monthly target (the "aggressive floor" rule). For each cluster, July total = Jun + max(0, Jun − May). This floors at June (no MoM decline allowed) and carries the absolute May→Jun growth forward in full. Clusters that softened in June stay anchored at June; clusters that grew get the full lift. Aggregate 20-cluster July benchmark = 5,764 orders, +12.2% vs Jun 5,137.

2. Daily distribution (DOW × week-of-month profile). Each cluster's monthly target is spread across the 31 July days using that cluster's own Mar-Jun day-of-week and week-of-month profile. Mondays sit at ~0.5× the daily average (week-off effect), Sundays at ~1.4×, weekdays in between. Last 3 days of the month carry the historical month-end push.

3. Iterative recalibration (starts when July begins). Each daily report, after day N, run-rate adjustment = actual_cumulative_through_N ÷ original_projection_through_N. Remaining days (N+1 to 31) are re-projected at this factor. If a cluster runs 10% ahead through the first week, the rest of the month projects 10% higher. If it slips 5%, the rest projects 5% lower. The revised monthly total updates every day with fresh data.

4. "To-go run-rate" (the chase number). = (original_target − actual_so_far) ÷ remaining_days. This is the orders-per-day a cluster needs to maintain across remaining days to still hit its original benchmark. Compare to the actual run-rate observed so far to know whether to push harder or cruise.

Cluster scope: 23 clusters. Metric defs per current spec (MS total, MD FS+Insta from First MD for Fresh + Last MD for Reactivation, Orders/HOTO FS+Insta by Current_Sales_Channel). BQL from BQL raw file, summed by Action_Date and city. Targets from Sales Planning sheet.
